seoTitle: How to Save, Import & Export Templates in Elementor
metaDescription: Learn how to save, export as JSON, and import Elementor templates between WordPress sites, plus how to fix templates that won't import correctly.
intro: Reusing page designs across WordPress sites, or simply backing up your layouts, is much easier once you know how Elementor's template system works. This guide covers saving a page as a template, exporting it as a JSON file, and importing it into another WordPress site. It works with both Elementor Free and Elementor Pro, and also covers fixing templates that fail to import or come in missing widgets.

steps.heading: Save your page as a template
steps.body: In Elementor, save the current page design as a template so it's stored in your Template Library for reuse.

steps.heading: Export the template as a JSON file
steps.body: From the Template Library, export the saved template, which downloads it as a JSON file you can move between sites.

steps.heading: Import the template into another WordPress site
steps.body: On the new site, open Elementor's Template Library and use the import option to upload the JSON file.

steps.heading: Fix template import or missing widget issues
steps.body: If a template fails to import or widgets appear missing, check that the destination site has the same plugins installed as the original.

steps.heading: Organize templates in your library
steps.body: Keep your Template Library organized with clear names and categories so saved designs are easy to find and reuse later.

faq.question: Does exporting Elementor templates work with the free version?
faq.answer: Yes, both Elementor Free and Elementor Pro support saving and exporting templates as JSON files.

faq.question: Why is my imported Elementor template missing widgets?
faq.answer: This usually happens when the destination site is missing a plugin or Elementor Pro widget that the original template relied on.

faq.question: Can I import an Elementor template into a completely different theme?
faq.answer: Generally yes, since Elementor templates are largely theme-independent, though some theme-specific styling may look different.

faq.question: How do I move my template library to a new website in bulk?
faq.answer: You can export multiple templates as JSON files individually and import them one by one, or use Elementor's bulk import option if available in your version.
